justifications.clarity: Section headings and parallel structure for the three fairness criteria make navigation and comparison easy; the opening book-arc recap grounds all subsequent content.
justifications.rigor: All key claims are supported by citations (Lopez-Lira, Chouldechova, Balogh & Didisheim); the RRF formula, fairness criteria, and DAG definition are formally correct; the calibrated uncertainty open problem could more precisely reference the conformal prediction framework.
justifications.completeness: All eight learning objectives are substantively addressed; all six chapter sections are covered; examples include the EDGAR monitor workflow, OpenClaw skills catalogue, fairness impossibility application, and the three-disciplines synthesis.
justifications.pedagogy: Eight objectives stated at the outset; each major section opens with a motivating example; difficulty progresses from survey to implementation to governance to frontier; the three-disciplines conclusion provides a memorable synthesis.
justifications.style: The path-forward closing has a distinctive voice; notation is consistent throughout; a few passages in Sections 4-5 lean heavily on bullet lists where prose would integrate ideas more naturally.
